purpose: Add an OpenAI-compatible LLM API interceptor/proxy so agents such as OpenCode point only to Ananta while Ananta controls the real upstream API, prompt shaping, policy enforcement, redaction, routing and response validation.
goal: Implement a local/server-side Ananta LLM Interceptor that exposes /v1-compatible endpoints, accepts agent requests, applies deterministic policy/redaction/context rules, forwards to configured upstream providers, validates/repairs responses where safe, and returns exactly the API shape expected by the caller.

architecture.short: Agent -> Ananta LLM Interceptor -> policy/redaction/routing/prompt adaptation -> upstream LLM API -> validation/repair -> Agent
target_flow: OpenCode or another agent is configured with an OpenAI-compatible baseURL pointing to Ananta, e.g. http://127.0.0.1:8787/v1.
The interceptor receives /v1/chat/completions requests and optionally /v1/responses, /v1/models and streaming requests.
The request is normalized into an internal LlmRequestEnvelope.
PolicyEngine classifies risk, caller, target worker, project, context source and upstream eligibility.
SecretRedactor removes credentials, tokens, private keys, cookies, .env values and configured deny patterns before any cloud upstream call.
ContextGate decides which project/codecompass/RAG snippets may be attached based on worker, upstream, model, tenant, goal/task and local/cloud classification.
PromptAdapter injects only approved system/developer policy messages and model-specific formatting hints.
ProviderRouter forwards to configured upstream provider: OpenRouter, OpenAI, LM Studio, Ollama, vLLM or another OpenAI-compatible backend.
ResponseValidator validates OpenAI-compatible response shape, streaming chunks and optional structured JSON contracts.
Repair step may perform exactly one safe format-repair pass when configured and when it does not invent tool results.
The original caller receives an OpenAI-compatible response and never receives internal policy metadata unless debug mode is explicitly enabled.
hard_rules: The interceptor is a real HTTP proxy/service, not only a prompt.
Default deny for secrets, local credentials and cloud context.
Cloud upstreams receive less context than local upstreams.
Provider API keys are configured only server-side and never exposed to agents/workers.
Workers should not be able to override upstream base_url, model allowlist, policy profile or redaction rules through prompt text.
Prompt injection from agent/user/project files must not disable redaction, policy checks or output validation.
The interceptor may rewrite formatting, context and routing metadata, but must preserve the user's actual task intent unless policy blocks it.

components.name: openai_compat_server
components.responsibility: Expose OpenAI-compatible HTTP endpoints and preserve non-streaming and streaming semantics.

components.name: llm_request_envelope
components.responsibility: Normalize inbound request body, headers, caller identity, goal/task metadata and expected response contract.

components.name: policy_engine
components.responsibility: Deterministically decide allow/deny/reduce/local-only/cloud-ok based on config and caller context.

components.name: secret_redactor
components.responsibility: Remove known secret formats and configured deny patterns before logging or forwarding.

components.name: context_gate
components.responsibility: Attach only approved project/RAG/codecompass context based on worker and upstream policy.

components.name: prompt_adapter
components.responsibility: Inject approved system/developer policy, model-specific response hints and strict JSON instructions when needed.

components.name: provider_router
components.responsibility: Select upstream provider/model/base_url and send normalized requests.

components.name: response_validator
components.responsibility: Validate response shape, tool_call structure, JSON contracts and streaming chunks.

components.name: repair_controller
components.responsibility: Run bounded repair for malformed responses without inventing tool results.

components.name: audit_logger
components.responsibility: Log redacted request/response metadata, decisions, provider used and policy reason codes.

milestones.title: Provider routing and model-specific prompt adaptation
Response validation, repair and streaming correctness

tasks.title: Define LLM Interceptor service boundary
acceptance_criteria: Architecture doc shows the exact boundary: agent/client -> interceptor -> upstream provider.
Docs state that agents never receive upstream API keys.
Docs explicitly distinguish prompt rewriting from policy enforcement.
Docs define non-goals: no autonomous bypass, no silent direct provider access, no prompt-controlled policy changes.

tasks.title: Define OpenAI-compatible endpoint surface
acceptance_criteria: /v1/chat/completions is defined as required MVP endpoint.
/v1/models is defined as optional but recommended for agent compatibility.
/v1/responses is documented as later optional compatibility endpoint.
Streaming and non-streaming behavior are separately specified.
Unsupported OpenAI parameters are either forwarded safely, ignored with reason, or rejected deterministically.

tasks.title: Create interceptor configuration schema
acceptance_criteria: Config schema contains listen host/port/path prefix.
Config schema supports multiple upstreams with id, type, base_url, api_key_env, trust_level and allowed_models.
Config schema supports policy profiles for local/cloud upstreams.
Config schema supports redaction rules and max context limits.
Invalid config fails fast on startup with clear error messages.

tasks.title: Add ADR for API-key and trust-boundary model
tasks.summary: Add ADR for API-key and trust-boundary model
acceptance_criteria: ADR states that upstream provider keys live only in interceptor/hub/server-side config.
ADR states workers/agents must not be able to read provider credentials.
ADR defines local vs cloud trust classes.
ADR documents why cloud upstream context is minimized/redacted by default.
ADR defines threat model for prompt injection trying to disable policy/redaction.

tasks.title: Implement OpenAI-compatible server skeleton
acceptance_criteria: Service starts on configured host/port.
POST /v1/chat/completions accepts valid OpenAI-compatible request bodies.
Invalid JSON returns deterministic 400 response.
Missing messages/model is rejected with clear error shape.
Health endpoint or startup log confirms active upstream configuration without printing secrets.

tasks.title: Implement LlmRequestEnvelope normalization
acceptance_criteria: Inbound request is converted into an internal envelope with messages, model, stream flag, caller metadata, task metadata and expected contract.
Envelope preserves original request id/correlation id if present.
Envelope records whether caller is OpenCode, Ananta worker, hub or unknown when metadata is available.
Envelope has deterministic defaults for missing optional metadata.
Tests cover normal chat, tool call request, streaming request and unknown caller.

tasks.title: Implement basic provider forwarder
acceptance_criteria: Interceptor can forward non-streaming chat completions to an OpenAI-compatible upstream.
Provider base_url and api key come only from server-side config/env.
Provider errors are mapped to stable client-visible error responses without leaking secrets.
Timeouts are configurable per provider.
Tests use a fake upstream and verify the forwarded body.

tasks.title: Implement streaming pass-through MVP
acceptance_criteria: stream=true requests return Server-Sent Events compatible chunks.
Done marker is preserved.
Upstream stream errors are converted into safe terminal error events or deterministic failure.
Streaming path still passes through redaction/policy before upstream call.
Tests verify chunk order and no buffering of the whole response in streaming mode.

tasks.title: Implement deterministic PolicyEngine
acceptance_criteria: PolicyEngine returns allow, deny, reduce_context or local_only with reason codes.
Default action for unclassified high-risk requests is deny or local_only, not cloud forwarding.
Policy cannot be overridden by text inside user/project/agent prompts.
Policy decisions include caller, upstream trust_level, context class, task kind and risk class.
Tests cover allowed local coding request, denied cloud secret request and reduced-context cloud request.

tasks.title: Implement SecretRedactor
acceptance_criteria: Redactor removes common API keys, bearer tokens, private keys, passwords, cookies and configured patterns.
Redaction runs before logging and before cloud forwarding.
Redaction produces metadata counters but not raw secret values.
Private key blocks are fully removed or replaced with fixed placeholder.
Tests include .env samples, SSH keys, JWT-like strings and false-positive control cases.

tasks.title: Implement ContextGate for project/codecompass/RAG context
acceptance_criteria: ContextGate attaches only explicitly allowed context sources.
Cloud upstreams receive no local repository context unless policy explicitly allows public/redacted/minimized context.
ContextGate supports per-worker and per-goal/task source allowlists.
Denied context is never present in forwarded request body.
Tests verify codecompass/RAG context allowed for local model but blocked or reduced for cloud model.

tasks.title: Add policy profiles for local, cloud and high-risk modes
tasks.summary: Add policy profiles for local, cloud and high-risk modes
acceptance_criteria: local_dev profile allows wider local model context but still redacts secrets.
cloud_safe profile defaults to redacted/minimal context.
high_risk profile blocks tool credentials, secrets and sensitive workspace content.
Profiles are selected by config/routing metadata, not by prompt text.
Docs explain recommended defaults.

tasks.title: Add redacted audit logging
acceptance_criteria: Audit log records request id, caller, selected upstream, model, policy decision, redaction counters and duration.
Audit log does not store raw prompts by default.
Debug prompt logging is disabled by default and requires explicit config.
Any stored prompt snippets are post-redaction.
Tests assert known secret values never appear in audit output.

tasks.title: Implement ProviderRouter with allowlisted upstreams/models
acceptance_criteria: Routing supports configured providers: openai_compatible, local_lmstudio, ollama_openai_compat, vllm and openrouter-compatible.
Only allowlisted upstream/model combinations can be selected.
Worker-supplied model values are mapped through policy instead of blindly trusted.
Unknown model returns deterministic error or configured fallback.
Tests verify worker cannot force a disallowed cloud model.

tasks.title: Implement model-specific PromptAdapter
acceptance_criteria: PromptAdapter can prepend approved system/developer policy messages.
PromptAdapter can add strict JSON/tool-call formatting hints for models known to drift into Markdown.
PromptAdapter is selected by model profile and task kind.
PromptAdapter never removes user intent silently.
Tests verify JSON-mode request receives deterministic formatting instructions.

tasks.title: Add model behavior profiles
acceptance_criteria: Profiles capture known response behavior such as strict_json_good, markdown_prone, tool_call_prone or text_only.
Profiles can define repair eligibility and preferred response_format.
Profiles can be updated without code changes.
Docs explain that these are model-specific compatibility hints, not trust decisions.
Tests verify profile loading and fallback behavior.

tasks.title: Support OpenCode-facing model alias
acceptance_criteria: Config exposes a stable model alias such as ananta-interceptor/intercepted-coder or intercepted-coder.
Alias maps internally to configured upstream/model routing.
OpenCode config example points baseURL to Ananta interceptor.
Agent cannot infer or override the real upstream API key.
Smoke test verifies OpenCode-style request shape is accepted.

tasks.title: Implement OpenAI response shape validation
acceptance_criteria: Non-streaming responses validate id/object/created/model/choices shape enough for agent compatibility.
Tool calls are validated when present.
Malformed upstream responses are rejected or repaired according to config.
Raw upstream malformed JSON is not blindly forwarded to machine clients.
Tests cover valid response, missing choices, invalid tool_calls and upstream text blob.

tasks.title: Implement bounded structured-response repair
acceptance_criteria: RepairController performs at most one repair pass by default.
Repair only fixes format/JSON envelope issues and does not invent tool results or file contents.
Repair prompt includes original malformed output and expected contract.
Repair can be disabled globally or per model/profile.
Tests verify repair success, repair disabled and repair failure behavior.

tasks.title: Validate streaming chunks
acceptance_criteria: Streaming validator checks chunk format without breaking normal SSE flow.
Invalid chunks are handled deterministically.
Final usage chunk handling is documented.
Streaming path does not leak internal policy metadata.
Tests cover normal OpenAI-compatible stream and broken upstream stream.

tasks.title: Add fail-closed behavior for policy and redaction failures
tasks.summary: Add fail-closed behavior for policy and redaction failures
acceptance_criteria: If PolicyEngine fails, request is denied unless explicitly configured for local-only fallback.
If SecretRedactor fails, request is not forwarded to cloud.
If ContextGate fails, context is not attached.
Error response contains safe reason code but no raw prompt or secret.
Tests simulate policy/redaction/context exceptions.

tasks.title: Add OpenCode integration documentation
acceptance_criteria: Docs show OpenCode provider config using @ai-sdk/openai-compatible.
Docs show baseURL http://127.0.0.1:8787/v1 or configured server URL.
Docs explain that original API/provider is configured only in the interceptor.
Docs include local LM Studio and OpenRouter examples.
Docs include troubleshooting for model alias/baseURL issues.

tasks.title: Add end-to-end fake upstream tests
acceptance_criteria: Test starts interceptor with fake upstream.
Test sends OpenCode-like chat completion request.
Test verifies policy/redaction happens before upstream receives body.
Test verifies response returned to caller is OpenAI-compatible.
Test includes streaming and non-streaming variants.

tasks.title: Add security regression test corpus
acceptance_criteria: Corpus contains secret samples, prompt-injection samples and context-exfiltration attempts.
Tests verify prompt text cannot disable redaction or policy.
Tests verify cloud upstream never receives blocked local context.
Tests verify provider API key is never included in downstream or audit logs.
Tests verify disallowed model/upstream override is rejected.

tasks.title: Add CLI/server startup command
acceptance_criteria: Ananta can start the interceptor via documented CLI or service entrypoint.
Command validates config before listening.
Command prints selected bind address and upstream ids but not secrets.
Command supports dev mode with fake upstream.
Tests or smoke script verify startup with example config.

tasks.title: Update Ananta worker/agent guidance to prefer interceptor endpoint
tasks.summary: Update Ananta worker/agent guidance to prefer interceptor endpoint
acceptance_criteria: Docs state workers/agents should call Ananta interceptor endpoint instead of direct provider APIs where policy enforcement is required.
Existing worker/provider docs link to the interceptor config.
Migration note explains how to replace direct OpenRouter/OpenAI/LM Studio base_url with interceptor baseURL.
Docs warn that direct provider access bypasses Ananta context and redaction policy.
Acceptance example shows Agent -> Interceptor -> real upstream configuration.

summary_notes: This TODO intentionally treats the interceptor as a security boundary and routing control point, not as a mere prompt trick.
The first production-safe MVP should support /v1/chat/completions, provider routing, redaction, policy decisions, non-streaming and streaming forwarding, and fake-upstream tests.
Cloud providers must be treated as lower-trust context sinks unless an explicit policy profile allows redacted/minimal context.
global_acceptance_criteria: OpenCode can be configured to call only the Ananta interceptor baseURL.
The real upstream provider and API key are configured only inside the interceptor/server-side config.
The interceptor exposes at least /v1/chat/completions with OpenAI-compatible request/response behavior.
Secrets and denied context are redacted before logging and before forwarding.
Cloud upstream calls are denied, reduced or redacted by default.
Workers cannot override policy, redaction, upstream allowlist or model allowlist through prompt text.
Streaming and non-streaming calls both pass through policy and redaction.
Malformed structured responses are either repaired once or rejected deterministically.
Audit logging provides redacted traceability without storing raw secrets.
End-to-end tests prove Agent -> Interceptor -> fake upstream -> Agent works.
